description_long: This sculpture depicts a dramatic scene of a large prehistoric animal skeleton, likely a bear or similar mammal, posed upright with its front limbs gripping a tree trunk. The skeleton is mounted on a wooden base, which also features smaller fossilized skulls and bones displayed on individual stands. The main skeleton is highly detailed, showing ribcage, spine, limbs, and a prominent skull with open jaws. The tree trunk is textured and stands vertically, adding to the dynamic composition. The base is polygonal and includes small plaques or labels, suggesting a museum-style display. The overall appearance is that of a natural history exhibit, emphasizing paleontological themes.
description: A detailed sculpture of a large prehistoric animal skeleton gripping a tree, mounted on a wooden base with smaller fossil displays and museum-style plaques.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the full skeleton gripping the tree, with smaller fossils and plaques visible on the wooden base.
description_view_1: The rear view shows the spine, ribcage, and tail of the skeleton, as well as the rounded shape of the base and a large fossil at the back.
description_view_2: The opposite side view mirrors the first, showing the skeleton's profile, the tree, and the arrangement of smaller fossils.
description_view_3: The frontal view centers on the skeleton's skull and chest, with the tree trunk directly in front and smaller fossils partially obscured.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a wooden base with museum-style plaques, suggesting an indoor exhibit or educational setting.
